content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF SUTTER

DARRELL W. LARSEN
County Counsel

1160 Civic Center Boulevard
Yuba City, California 95993
Telephone (916) 741-7110

February 6, 1991

RONALD S. ERICKSON
Assistant

JAMES SCANLON
JOSEPH P. CERULLO
Deputies

Fair Political Practices
Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: COSTA REZONING

Gentlemen:

We have spoken with Attorney John Wallace of your Legal Division
concerning this matter. Pursuant to Government Code section
83114(b) and 2 CCR 18329, we are requesting on behalf of
Supervisor Peter Licari formal written advice concerning the
following matter. Peter Licari is a Sutter County Supervisor and
his mailing address is 1160 Civic Center Boulevard, Yuba City,
California 95993. He has authorized this office to make this
request for formal written advice on his behalf.

Properties owned by Manual Costa located in the unincorporated
community of Tierra Buena, northwest of Yuba City, have been
proposed for a general plan change and rezoning. Attached hereto
please find the staff report to the Planning Commission, minutes
of the Planning Commission consideration of the matter, and a
staff report to the Board of Supervisors on the same subject.
The site map associated with the staff report contains a slight
error in designation of the subject site which error we have
corrected. The subject site has frontage on both Butte House
Road and Elmer Avenue. Supervisor Licari's property is located
to the north of the subject site, is designated on the site map
that we have provided to you and has frontage on Elmer Avenue.
It is approximately five acres in area and contains Mr. Licari's
residence.

As you can see from the staff report to the Board of Supervisors,
Planning staff is recommending to the Board that rather than
rezoning the property of Mr. Costa to a commercial designation,
the suggestion of staff is that it be rezoned to multi-family
residential. Multi-family residential zoning would be consistent
with the medium density residential design placed on the
property by the applicable general plan. Mr. Licari's property
is designated low density residential in that same plan and is
presently zoned R-2 to Z CCR R-1. To the extent it is of interest to you, the
Sutter County Assessor informs this office that a 1.95 acre
parcel zoned for commercial purposes (C-2) located on Butte House
Road just west of Redding Avenue and the railroad tracks sold for

Fair Political Practices
Commission
February 6, 1991
Page Two

a land-only price of $3.23 per square foot. A 12,000 square foot
lot sold somewhat to the west of the first parcel at a price for
land only of $3.50 per square foot in May of 1990. A local
realtor has informed us that many residential lots are available
for sale in Yuba City at $35,000 per lot.

The Sutter County Planning Department informs us that they would
view as the "neighborhood" in which the subject and Mr. Licari's
properties are located as being bounded by the railroad which is
shown in the Tierra Buena General Plan on both the west and south
sides of the area in question, the city limits of Yuba City which
are just east of Blevin Road on the east, and Young Road and its
extensions on the north. The character of the neighborhood is
described by the Planning Department as rural residential. The
Planning Department does not believe that there is necessarily a
correlation between land uses on Mr. Costa's property and those
on Supervisor Licari's. This is because the General Plan has
already drawn a distinction between the two properties,
designating Mr. Costa's for medium density residential uses and
Mr. Licari's for low density residential use. With regard to the
financial impact on Supervisor Licari's property of any of the
contemplated changes on Mr. Costa's property, the Planning
Department is unable to reach any conclusions, believing that
evaluation of any such financial impact would be speculative.
Should Mr. Costa's proposal be approved and the land developed
for commercial offices, the traffic impacts would be primarily
confined to Butte House Road and the southerly portion of Elmer
Avenue. That is, additional traffic might be introduced on Butte
House Road near Mr. Costa's property and similarly on Elmer Road
in the immediate vicinity of Mr. Costa's property. It would not
be anticipated that traffic increases generated by Mr. Costa's
property would travel northerly. Rather, it would be expected
that they would travel east and south, to and from Yuba City.

Supervisor Licari, of course, would like to know from you whether
he may participate in the discussions and decision to be made by
the Sutter County Board of Supervisors with regard to Mr. Costa's
request. If we can be of further assistance, please do not
hesitate to call upon us.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

DARRELL W. LARSEN
SUTTER COUNTY COUNSEL

DWL;js

Enclosures

cc: Supervisor Peter Licari

sections.question_synthetic: May Supervisor Peter Licari participate in discussions and decisions regarding a proposed rezoning of nearby property owned by Manuel Costa, given that Licari's own property is adjacent to and could potentially be affected by the rezoning decision?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal question or conclusion sections are present in the document. The legal question is embedded in the closing paragraph. Document references Government Code section 83114(b) and 2 CCR 18329 regarding formal written advice request.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: May Supervisor Peter Licari participate in discussions and decisions regarding a proposed rezoning of nearby property owned by Manuel Costa, given that Licari's own property is adjacent to and could potentially be affected by the rezoning decision?

embedding.summary: Sutter County Counsel requests formal written advice from the FPPC on behalf of Supervisor Peter Licari regarding whether he may participate in Board of Supervisors discussions and decisions on a proposed general plan change and rezoning of nearby property owned by Manuel Costa. The letter details the properties' locations, characteristics, and potential impacts.
